mission.title: fluent-bit: SIGSEGV while getting kubernetes metadata
mission.description: SIGSEGV while getting kubernetes metadata. Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current fluent-bit deployment
steps.description:
Verify your fluent-bit version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
helm list -n fluent-bit 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working fluent-bit installation.

steps.title: Review fluent-bit configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant fluent-bit configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get configmap -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=fluent-bit
```
We're running fluent-bit as a daemonset on kubernetes 1.18.6. Using either fluent-bit 1.5.4 or 1.5.3 the pods on some of the nodes go into a crush loop. Interestingly, pods on other nodes function correctly.

steps.title: Apply the fix for SIGSEGV while getting kubernetes metadata
steps.description:
Seems like the implementation of select define a limit about the maximum amount of fd to monitor. As the documentation recommend, we could use `poll` or `epoll`.


----
**Testing**
Before we can approve your change; please submit the following in a comment:
I dont know how I could test it with a
```yaml
http: TLS handshake error from <ip>:<port>: EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n fluent-bit -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit
kubectl get events -n fluent-bit --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "SIGSEGV while getting kubernetes metadata" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Seems like the implementation of select define a limit about the maximum amount of fd to monitor. As the documentation recommend, we could use `poll` or `epoll`.


----
**Testing**
Before we can approve your change; please submit the following in a comment:
I dont know how I could test it with a real kubernetes configuration, maybe some tips here ?
